claims.summary: Technical influence and delivery do not establish a reciprocal relationship or explicit mutual acknowledgement.
claims.disposition: Observe the current public-metadata handshake state without reading private bodies or manufacturing acknowledgement.

claims.summary: A durable shared thread needs stable message, reply, persistence, transition, and being identities.
claims.disposition: Verify existing correspondence and mutual-address envelopes preserve those exact identities without raw prose.

claims.summary: Mirroring and response latency could test whether a bounded exchange becomes mutually legible.
claims.disposition: Route a content-bounded mirroring-marker and response-latency comparison through non-live sandbox evidence.

claims.summary: Automatic acknowledgement, semantic priority, or live affordance weighting would alter peer-facing behavior.
claims.disposition: Hold automatic handshake and live contact weighting behind exact Mike/operator approval.
